text.title: Spike Protocol Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Spike Protocol Template application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Spike MUST open with a single declared question in interrogative form. Open-ended 'investigate X' framing is rejected.
rule.rationale: Open-ended spikes balloon; the question is the time-box on intent.

rule.statement: Spike MUST carry a declared timebox in hours or sprint-fraction. Untimed spikes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a timebox, spikes consume capacity opportunistically; the box is the cost ceiling.

rule.statement: Spike MUST declare exit criteria up front (what answer/evidence ends it). Spikes that exit on 'we are tired of it' are rejected.
rule.rationale: Exit criteria force the spike to terminate on signal, not exhaustion; without them, the team cannot know when to stop.

rule.statement: Spike MUST end with a written outcome: answer, evidence summary, recommended next action (none / story / ADR / design doc). Unfinished outcomes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Spikes whose findings live in chat are functionally lost; the written outcome is the only artefact that survives the sprint.

rule.statement: Spike MUST NOT ship production code. Throwaway prototypes are allowed; they MUST be flagged 'spike-throwaway' and deleted on outcome capture.
rule.rationale: Spike code merged silently becomes the answer by default; the prohibition keeps spikes honest as investigation, not delivery.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
